title: Data Leakage Audit
objective: Identify features, split strategies, and preprocessing choices that could leak target or cohort information.
method: Combined documentation review with empirical checks of missingness, site prevalence, and target-adjacent variables.
results: Four leakage risks were identified: site leakage, site-coded missingness, procedure-adjacent variables, and random split optimism.
Site disease prevalence ranges from 36.05% to 93.50%.
warnings: Missing values and site-level sampling differences may affect all estimates.
Leakage risk depends on the intended clinical prediction moment, which is not fully specified by the dataset.
